commentary: Eleven activities this week versus ten last week, which honestly barely counts as an improvement until you look at what actually happened inside it — two-a-days on Saturday and Sunday, back to back, like the body just decided rest was a suggestion from someone else. The swim volume is strong, sitting at 8,156 metres across five sessions, and that Saturday morning 2,050 metres at 149 bpm tells a very different story than the dreamy 95 bpm B&B float from last Sunday, which was basically a spa day with goggles. The run dropped from 13.7 kilometres to 13.1 and the heart rate came down with it, which could mean better fitness or could mean the legs were quietly negotiating with the rest of the body after all those doubles. Either way, he showed up every single day this week, which is the kind of thing that looks effortless from the outside and absolutely is not.
